briefdescription.para: Grabs snapshot of previous state when the view is highlighted.
Register property notification to check highlighted object position.
Remove property notification added by RegisterPropertyNotification.

Registers PropertySet signal to notify when ACCESSIBILITY_NAME or ACCESSIBILITY_DESCRIPTION is changed. Note that those two signals only need for highlighted view. So, let us ensure to connect PropertySet signal only if view has been grabbed.
Unregisters PropertySet signal to notify when ACCESSIBILITY_NAME or ACCESSIBILITY_DESCRIPTION is changed.
Check if the actor is showing.

simplesect.para: True if the actor is showing

briefdescription.para: Returns the actor's name in the absence of ACCESSIBILITY_NAME property.

simplesect.para: A pair containing the name and a boolean indicating if empty names are allowed

briefdescription.para: Returns the actor's description in the absence of ACCESSIBILITY_DESCRIPTION property.
Function to set a custom highlight overlay.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets a custom highlight overlay at the specified position and size.

parameterdescription.para: A Vector2 representing the position of the overlay
A Vector2 representing the size of the overlay

briefdescription.para: Function to reset the custom highlight overlay.

detaileddescription.para: This function resets the custom highlight overlay.

briefdescription.para: Makes sure that a given child (descendant) of this container (e.g. ItemView) is visible.

simplesect.para: false if scrolling is not supported or child is already visible

briefdescription.para: Returns the index of the property that represents this actor's name.
Returns the index of the property that represents this actor's description.
Sets last object position.
Gets last object position.
Handles AcessibilityState property change; Only called when the view is highlighted.
Returns true if given actor is considered as modal by propeties set.
Returns true if given actor is considered as 3D scene view by propeties set.
Appliys relavant accessibility properties to AT-SPI states.
Represents the Accessible object for Dali::Ui::View and derived classes.

detaileddescription.para: You can create a derived class (and override View::CreateAccessibleObject) in order to customize Accessibility for a given view.
